key_points: The concept of 'Satyagraha' was not just passive resistance but a proactive search for truth, suggesting that if the cause was true, physical force was unnecessary.
The Khilafat Movement (led by Shaukat and Muhammad Ali) was integrated with Non-Cooperation to bring Muslims into the fold of the national struggle.
The Non-Cooperation Movement took different forms: peasants in Awadh fought against 'talukdars,' while tribals in Gudem Hills used militant methods under Alluri Sitaram Raju.
Business classes supported the Civil Disobedience Movement through the FICCI, seeking protection against imports and a favorable rupee-sterling exchange rate.
The 'limitations' of the movement included the lukewarm participation of Dalits and some Muslim organizations due to concerns over future political representation.
Nationalism was fostered culturally through folklore, icons, and the reinterpretation of history to prove India’s past greatness before colonial rule.
examples: Peasant Struggles: Baba Ramchandra leading the Oudh Kisan Sabha against high rents and 'begar' (unpaid labor).
Tribal Resistance: Alluri Sitaram Raju claiming special powers and leading a guerrilla warfare movement in Andhra Pradesh.
Industrial Participation: Workers in Nagpur and railway workers participating in strikes and boycotts.
Women’s Role: Thousands of women from high-caste families in cities and rich peasant households in villages joined Gandhi’s marches.
formulas: Khilafat + Non-Cooperation = Hindu-Muslim Unity + Mass Mobilization
Economic Impact = Boycott of Foreign Cloth + Rise of Indian Handlooms/Mills
Gandhi-Irwin Pact (1931) = Suspension of Civil Disobedience + Participation in Second Round Table Conference
exam_tips: Explain how 'Swaraj' meant different things to different people (e.g., for plantation workers, it meant the right to move freely).
Analyze the role of the 'Simon Commission' in consolidating Indian opposition because it lacked any Indian members.
Understand the significance of the 'Lord Irwin’s Offer' (Dominion Status) and why it was rejected by radicals like Nehru and Bose.
Be prepared to explain the 'Sense of Collective Belonging' through the use of Bharat Mata and national flags.
